title: Lesson Quiz: Compound Interest and Long-Term Growth
description: Assessment of the concept that Time value of money and exponential growth.

questions.prompt: What is compound interest or compound growth?

choices.text: Growth that builds on both the original amount and earlier growth
choices.feedback: Correct. Compound growth builds on previous growth as well as the starting amount.

choices.text: A one-time fee charged on an account
choices.feedback: A fee is not the same as compound growth.

choices.text: Growth that happens only in one single month
choices.feedback: Compound growth can continue over time rather than only once.

choices.text: A type of tax on investments
choices.feedback: Compound growth is not a tax.

questions.prompt: Time can strengthen long-term investment growth.

choices.feedback: Time can strengthen growth because it gives value more opportunity to build.
Correct. Longer time periods can lead to stronger growth outcomes.

questions.prompt: If $800 grows to $920, how much growth occurred?

choices.feedback: Subtract the starting amount from the later amount carefully.
This total is too high for the values in the comparison.
The growth amount is lower than $180.

questions.prompt: Why can earlier investing produce stronger long-term outcomes?

choices.text: Because earlier investing gives growth more time to build
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reason, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Because earlier investing removes all uncertainty
choices.feedback: Earlier investing does not remove all uncertainty.

choices.text: Because earlier investing makes planning unnecessary
choices.feedback: Planning is still important in investment decisions.

choices.text: Because earlier investing gives growth more time to build
choices.feedback: Correct. Earlier investing can strengthen long-term growth because time allows more value to build.

questions.prompt: Long-term growth should be judged only by the starting amount and not by the time invested.

choices.feedback: Time is an important factor in long-term investment growth.
Correct. Long-term growth should consider both the amount invested and the time available for growth.
